hero_subtitle: SFTY helps regulated digital asset businesses assess existing providers and workflows to define phased, audit-ready compliance deployment with role-based controls and evidence integrity.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Implementation slows when scope is unclear
problem_description: Institutional compliance teams face delays and operational friction when existing providers, workflows and data sources are not fully mapped or assessed. This lack of clarity impedes effective deployment planning, weakens governance and complicates audit readiness for regulated VASPs.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY organizes existing provider outputs, operational workflows and data sources into one unified framework so compliance teams can assess scope clearly and plan enterprise-ready deployments with audit evidence.
workflow_title: From provider assessment to compliance readiness
workflow_description: SFTY maps existing provider outputs and workflows into structured review steps supporting role-based access, audit evidence and scalable compliance readiness for VASPs.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: More than assessment. Clear implementation scope
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides VASPs a provider-neutral compliance layer that connects existing tools into traceable workflows, decisions, evidence and audit-ready operational control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports mature VASPs with controlled provider orchestration, evidence preservation and deployment governance to meet rigorous bank due diligence and internal compliance standards.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support enterprise readiness and compliance scope
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how compliance workflows map to phased implementation plans
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence supports bank due diligence reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Compare role-based access and governance for institutional deployment
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for institutional compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, screening, case management and reporting into one structured workflow for digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure compliance workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY integrates provider outputs, applies internal policies, routes reviews and creates cases with decision logs and evidence to enable consistent, auditable compliance processes across jurisdictions.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case histories and supporting evidence with audit logs to maintain a clear, reviewable trail for bank due diligence, audits and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects multiple existing providers and data sources into one operating layer without requiring replacement of underlying KYC, KYT, Travel Rule or screening systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing providers, structuring their outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports rather than replacing them.
final_title: Assess compliance readiness for phased deployment
final_description: Review how SFTY maps existing providers and workflows to support audit-ready, role-based compliance deployment for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
